# hetzner-robot_failover (Resource)

Routes a Hetzner **failover IP** to an active server (`POST /failover/{ip}`) — a floating IP you can move between dedicated servers (e.g. a stable endpoint for a k8s control plane or ingress).

~> Failover IPs are permanent and cannot be deleted; destroying this resource only stops Terraform managing the route — the IP keeps pointing at the last `active_server_ip`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "hetzner-robot_failover" "vip" {
  failover_ip      = "1.2.3.4"
  active_server_ip = "5.6.7.8" # move to another server's IP to fail over
}
```

Import by failover IP:

```shell
terraform import hetzner-robot_failover.vip 1.2.3.4
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `active_server_ip` (String) IP of the server the failover IP currently routes to
- `failover_ip` (String) The failover IP address

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `netmask` (String) Failover netmask
- `server_ip` (String) IP of the server the failover IP belongs to
- `server_number` (Number) Number of the server the failover IP belongs to
